data.narrative: The student completed Module 2 by reading structured logs as request timelines, fixing missing request context, normalizing expiration timestamps to UTC, and identifying logs as a trust boundary. The strongest work was recognizing log injection from the structure of the forged admin-login line and verifying the fix by proving the malicious newline stayed on one physical log line.

dimension_scores.observation: Formed testable hypotheses for missing INFO logs, timezone mismatch, and forged log entries before moving to fixes.
dimension_scores.evidence: Student wrote that ERROR logs passed while INFO request/response logs were filtered, that UTC/local mismatches made records temporarily invisible, and that the admin login was injected via unsanitized URL input.

dimension_scores.observation: Read logs as timelines using request context and spotted structural anomalies in forged logs.
dimension_scores.evidence: Student identified r-003 as the failed 404, r-004 as the slow DB write, and noticed the fake admin login was embedded in Bob's URL rather than a real request flow.

dimension_scores.observation: Verified fixes with concrete curl requests and log output, including timestamp normalization and log injection protection.
dimension_scores.evidence: Student showed expires_at 2026-05-24T00:30:00+05:30 returned as 2026-05-23T19:00:00, curl -i /r/NGcojW returned a redirect, and the malicious log payload printed on one physical line.

dimension_scores.observation: Communicated findings clearly in short incident-style statements, though formal stakeholder updates were not part of this module.
dimension_scores.evidence: Student summarized: logs are also an attack surface and user-controlled input must never be written directly into logs without sanitization or structured encoding.

dimension_scores.observation: Connected symptoms to systemic causes: logging configuration/absence, timezone convention mismatch, and log trust-boundary failure.
dimension_scores.evidence: Student wrote that logging must be environment-driven and that timezone bugs come from mixing naive datetime, UTC datetime, and local server time.

dimension_scores.observation: Reflection captured risks and mitigations but did not produce a full postmortem artifact.
dimension_scores.evidence: Student reflected that minimal logging needs security/audit retention, timestamps must be normalized to UTC, and log inputs must be sanitized.

strengths: Strong log literacy: followed request IDs and separated failures, latency, and forged events using log structure.
Strong security insight: recognized logs as an attack surface and verified newline sanitization prevented forged entries.
Good timestamp discipline: normalized an offset timestamp to a UTC-equivalent value and verified redirect behavior.
growth_areas: When changing configuration, preserve established config-loading patterns such as BaseSettings so .env behavior remains reliable.
data.recommendation: In Module 3, turn each intermittent symptom into a repeatable reproduction script before changing code; include the command and exact output as proof.

public_evidence.artifact: Request logging, UTC timestamp handling, and log sanitization in URL shortener API
public_evidence.student_action: Added environment-driven INFO request logging, normalized incoming expiration timestamps to UTC, and sanitized logged user-controlled URL values to prevent log injection.
public_evidence.technical_detail: API logging around request/response lifecycle; expires_at conversion from 2026-05-24T00:30:00+05:30 to UTC-equivalent 2026-05-23T19:00:00; CR/LF escaping in logged URL fields.
public_evidence.proof: Missing-link logs showed INFO request received, ERROR link not found, INFO response sent; curl -i /r/NGcojW returned a redirect; malicious newline payload stayed on one physical log line.
public_evidence.ability: Reads logs as timelines, verifies timestamp behavior, and protects log integrity
public_evidence.reviewer_value: Shows the student can improve observability and security evidence quality in a backend service using concrete verification.

concepts_demonstrated.evidence: Grouped events by request and explained failure/slowness from req_id and duration fields.
Verified a +05:30 timestamp became the UTC-equivalent stored/returned value.
Identified forged admin login inside user URL input and verified CR/LF sanitization kept it on one physical line.

teaching_approaches.detail: Asked for hypotheses and concrete verification before advancing each bug.
